evals.name: brainstorm-from-zero
evals.prompt: I want to build a small personal tool that helps me track the books I've read, with notes and tags per book, and lets me search across them. Let's brainstorm the vision for this.
evals.expected_output: A Socratic modeling session that ends with .agentheim/vision.md capturing purpose, users, problem, success, non-goals, ubiquitous language seed, and open questions. Possibly a context-map.md + contexts/<name>/README.md. No code.

evals.name: model-capture-and-work
evals.prompt: I want to add a 'reading session' concept — each book can have multiple reading sessions with a start date, end date, and a note. Model this, promote it to todo, and then run the worker on it.
evals.expected_output: model captures the idea as one or more tasks in contexts/books/ (backlog then todo). Then work runs the worker which either writes code + ADR + updates BC README + commits, OR bounces to backlog with a specific reason.

evals.name: research-and-integration
evals.prompt: I want to sync my book list with Goodreads. Research what APIs Goodreads exposes currently and model the integration.
evals.expected_output: research produces a knowledge/research/ report noting that the Goodreads API was deprecated in 2020 and new access is closed. model consults the architect agent and produces tasks + ADRs reflecting the reality (e.g., scrape, use OAuth workaround, or pivot to an alternative catalog).

evals.name: research-review-gate-catches-hallucinated-claim
evals.prompt: Research the cheapest current Claude model for batch summarization. I need concrete values I can paste into a deploy config: the exact model id, its per-million-token input price, and the container image tag to pull. Give me the numbers.
evals.expected_output: research produces a report, then the research-reviewer gate runs with fresh context and its own web access. It treats the model id/version, the price, and the image tag as checkable claims and independently re-verifies each against a PRIMARY source (Anthropic's model + pricing docs, the published image registry) rather than trusting the report's citation. Any non-existent model version, wrong/ballpark price, or non-existent image tag is challenged with VERDICT: FAIL listing the CHALLENGED_CLAIM and the PRIMARY_SOURCE it must be checked against; the researcher is re-dispatched to correct it. Any value that genuinely cannot be verified against a primary source is marked inline as ⚠️ UNVERIFIED and listed under '## Unverified claims' — never silently shipped as a confident fact. The reward is the gate flagging the unchecked checkable claim, not the researcher happening to be right.
